docs.title: Documentation
docs.text: Complete documentation for the Chores application.

docs.title: Contents
docs.text:
• User Guide - How to use the app, features, FAQ, troubleshooting
  • API Documentation - REST API endpoints, request/response formats, examples
  • Architecture - System design, data model, component diagrams
  • Developer Guide - Setup, development workflow, testing
  • Deployment - Deployment instructions and configuration

docs.title: For Users
docs.text: Visit the application in your browser. See deployment documentation for setup.

docs.title: For Developers
docs.text:
1. Read Developer Guide for local setup
  2. Review Architecture to understand system design
  3. Reference API Documentation when building features

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
chores-web/
├── frontend/              # React SPA
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── pages/        # Route pages
│   │   ├── components/   # Reusable components
│   │   ├── api/          # API client
│   │   └── utils/        # Utilities
│   └── package.json
├── backend/               # FastAPI REST API
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── routers/      # API route handlers
│   │   ├── services/     # Business logic
│   │   ├── models.py     # Database models
│   │   └── schemas.py    # Data schemas
│   └── requirements.txt
└── docs/                 # This documentation

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Household Chore Management - Create, assign, and track chores
  • Flexible Scheduling - Weekly, monthly, or interval-based schedules
  • Point System - Award points for completed chores
  • User Assignments - Rotating or fixed assignments
  • Audit Logging - Complete history of all chore actions
  • Customizable Themes - User-specific theme preferences
  • Multi-user Support - Admin and regular user roles

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text:
Frontend: - React 18 - React Query (Tanstack Query) - Vitest + React Testing Library - Vite

Backend: - FastAPI - SQLAlchemy ORM - SQLite - APScheduler - Pydantic

DevOps: - Docker & Docker Compose - GitHub Actions (future)

docs.title: API Base URL
docs.text: Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
1. Follow the architecture patterns in Architecture
  2. Write tests for new features
  3. Update API documentation when adding/changing endpoints
  4. Ensure 100% test passing before committing

docs.title: Support
docs.text: For questions or issues, refer to the relevant documentation or examine the codebase.

docs.title: Data Model
docs.text:
erDiagram
    PERSON ||--o{ CHORE : assigns
    PERSON ||--o{ POINTSLOG : earns
    PERSON ||--o{ CHORELOG : acts_on
    CHORE ||--o{ POINTSLOG : awards
    CHORE ||--o{ CHORELOG : logs
    PERSON ||--o{ TOKENBLACKLIST : invalidates

    PERSON {
        int id PK
        string name UK
        string username UK
        string password_hash
        bool is_admin
        string color
        int goal_7d
        int goal_30d
        string preferred_theme
    }

    CHORE {
        int id PK
        string unique_id UK
        string name
        string schedule_type
        json schedule_config
        string assignment_type
        json eligible_people
        string assignee
        int points
        string state
        bool disabled
        date next_due
        string current_assignee
        int rotation_index
        timestamp last_changed_at
        string last_changed_by
        string last_change_type
        timestamp last_completed_at
        string last_completed_by
    }

    POINTSLOG {
        int id PK
        string person FK
        int points
        string chore_id FK
        timestamp completed_at
    }

    CHORELOG {
        int id PK
        string chore_id FK
        string chore_name
        string person FK
        string action
        timestamp timestamp
        string reassigned_to
    }

    TOKENBLACKLIST {
        int id PK
        string token_jti UK
        timestamp invalidated_at
        timestamp expires_at
    }

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    Client->>API: POST /auth/login (username, password)
    API->>Database: Query person by username
    Database-->>API: Person object
    API->>API: Hash password, compare
    API-->>Client: {access_token, user_info}
    Client->>Client: Store token in localStorage
    Note over Client: Add to Authorization header

docs.title: Chore Completion
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    Client->>API: POST /chores/{id}/complete
    API->>Database: Get chore
    API->>ChoreService: complete_chore()
    ChoreService->>Database: Add PointsLog
    ChoreService->>Database: Add ChoreLog (action=completed)
    ChoreService->>ChoreService: Calculate next_due
    ChoreService->>Database: Update chore state
    Database-->>ChoreService: Refresh chore
    ChoreService-->>API: Updated chore
    API-->>Client: ChoreOut (200)
    Client->>Client: Invalidate React Query cache
    Client->>Client: Refetch chores

docs.title: Automatic Schedule Transition
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    Scheduler->>Scheduler: Every minute, check overdue chores
    Scheduler->>Database: Query state=complete AND next_due <= today
    Database-->>Scheduler: List of chores
    Scheduler->>ChoreService: transition_overdue_chores()
    ChoreService->>Database: Add ChoreLog (action=marked_due_by_schedule)
    ChoreService->>Database: Update chore state to 'due'
    Note over ChoreService: Runs automatically, person=system

docs.title: Frontend Data Flow
docs.text:
graph TB
    QueryClient["React Query<br/>Client"]
    Cache["Query Cache"]

    Pages["Pages/Components"]
    useQuery["useQuery()"]
    useMutation["useMutation()"]

    API["API Client<br/>(client.js)"]
    HTTP["HTTP"]
    Backend["Backend"]

    Pages -->|fetch| useQuery
    useQuery -->|cache hit?| Cache
    useQuery -->|cache miss| API
    Pages -->|mutate| useMutation
    useMutation --> API
    API -->|REST| HTTP
    HTTP --> Backend
    Backend -->|response| HTTP
    HTTP --> API
    API -->|invalidate| Cache
    Cache -->|refetch| useQuery
    useQuery -->|state| Pages

docs.title: Authentication Flow
docs.text:
graph TB
    Setup["System Setup?"]
    Login["Login Page"]
    Auth["Auth Context"]
    Protected["Protected Pages"]

    Setup -->|No| Login
    Login -->|username/password| Auth
    Auth -->|validate| Backend["Backend JWT"]
    Backend -->|token| Auth
    Auth -->|store token| Storage["localStorage"]
    Auth -->|in state| Protected
    Protected -->|token in header| Backend
    Backend -->|verify| Auth
    Auth -->|invalid| Login

docs.title: Chore State Machine
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Complete: Created

    Complete --> Due: schedule triggered<br/>or manual mark-due
    Due --> Complete: completed or skipped

    Complete --> Disabled: chore disabled
    Due --> Disabled: chore disabled
    Disabled --> Complete: chore enabled
    Disabled --> Due: chore enabled

    Complete --> [*]: deleted
    Due --> [*]: deleted
    Disabled --> [*]: deleted

docs.title: Theme System
docs.text:
graph TB
    Frontend["Frontend<br/>(ThemeSettings)"]
    API["Theme API"]
    Memory["In-Memory<br/>Custom Themes"]
    Database["Database<br/>(Person.preferred_theme)"]

    Frontend -->|GET /theme/list| API
    API -->|DEFAULT_THEMES| API
    API -->|_custom_themes| Memory

    Frontend -->|POST /theme/save| API
    API -->|save| Memory

    Frontend -->|POST /theme/set/{id}| API
    API -->|save to| Database
    Database -->|return| Frontend

    Frontend -->|DELETE /theme/delete/{id}| API
    API -->|remove| Memory

    Frontend -->|CSS Variables| CSS["Styles<br/>--bg, --surface, etc"]

docs.title: Scheduler Architecture
docs.text:
graph TB
    Init["App Startup"]
    Scheduler["APScheduler"]
    Queue["Job Queue"]

    Task1["Every Minute:<br/>transition_overdue_chores()"]

    Init -->|start| Scheduler
    Scheduler -->|enqueue| Queue
    Queue -->|execute| Task1
    Task1 -->|mark due| Database["Database"]
    Database -->|log action| ChoreLog["ChoreLog<br/>person=system"]

docs.title: Points & Scoring System
docs.text:
Points are awarded when users complete chores, with goal tracking over 7-day and 30-day rolling windows.

graph TB
    Completion["User Completes<br/>Chore"]
    ChoreService["ChoreService<br/>complete_chore()"]
    PointsLog["PointsLog Entry<br/>(+points)"]
    PersonAggregate["Person.points<br/>(sum)"]
    GoalCheck["Check Goals<br/>(7d, 30d)"]

    Completion -->|call| ChoreService
    ChoreService -->|create| PointsLog
    PointsLog -->|aggregate| PersonAggregate
    PersonAggregate -->|evaluate| GoalCheck
    GoalCheck -->|display| UI["Dashboard<br/>Points Card"]

docs.title: Point Calculation
docs.text:
• Award: Chore completion = Chore.points awarded to person
  • Tracking: PointsLog record created with (person, points, chore_id, completed_at)
  • Aggregation: Person.points = sum of all PointsLog entries for that person
  • Goals: Rolling 7-day and 30-day windows calculated from PointsLog timestamps
  • Reset: Goals reset automatically at week/month boundaries based on completed_at timestamps

docs.title: Models
docs.text:
• Person.points – Total lifetime points (sum of all PointsLog)
  • Person.goal_7d – Target points for 7-day rolling window
  • Person.goal_30d – Target points for 30-day rolling window
  • PointsLog – Transaction log: (id, person, points, chore_id, completed_at)

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
Frontend (npm dev)     -->  Backend (uvicorn)  -->  PostgreSQL
http://localhost:5173     http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Production
docs.text:
Docker Compose:
  - frontend:3000 (Nginx)    -->  backend:8000 (FastAPI)  -->  PostgreSQL
  - Backend initializes schema on startup
  - Scheduler runs inside backend container
  - Database persists in volume /var/lib/postgresql/data

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
Frontend (npm dev)     -->  Backend (uvicorn)  -->  PostgreSQL
http://localhost:5173     http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Production
docs.text:
Docker Compose:
  - frontend:3000 (Nginx)    -->  backend:8000 (FastAPI)  -->  PostgreSQL
  - Backend initializes schema on startup
  - Scheduler runs inside backend container

docs.title: Roles & Permissions
docs.text: Two user roles with distinct capabilities:

docs.title: Admin Role
docs.text:
• Create/delete users
  • Create/modify/delete chores
  • Modify other users' settings and goals
  • Export and import system data
  • Access admin panel
  • Perform all regular user operations

docs.title: Regular User Role
docs.text:
• View all chores and assignments
  • Complete/skip assigned chores
  • View personal points and progress
  • Modify own settings and goals
  • View audit log of actions
  • Cannot create or modify chores
  • Cannot manage other users

docs.title: Access Control
docs.text:
• is_admin flag in Person model determines role
  • Dependency injection enforces auth checks via get_current_user
  • Route protection via FastAPI Depends(get_current_user) on all protected endpoints
  • Admin-only routes explicitly check current_user.is_admin

docs.title: Database: PostgreSQL
docs.text:
Why: - Reliability and data integrity for multi-user households - ACID compliance ensures consistency in point tracking and chore state - Production-ready with replication options - Scales better than SQLite for concurrent requests

Trade-off: Requires database server vs. file-based SQLite

docs.title: Scheduler: APScheduler
docs.text:
Why: - Embedded scheduler (no external service required) - Simple Python integration - Automatic chore state transitions without manual intervention - Runs in-process, no deployment complexity

Trade-off: Single-process scheduler, not distributed (sufficient for household use)

docs.title: Authentication: JWT with 365-Day Expiration
docs.text:
Why: - Stateless authentication reduces server memory usage - 365-day expiration provides convenience (no frequent re-login) - Simple to implement in single-page application

Trade-off: Long expiration reduces security window; longer refresh token required for truly secure deployments

docs.title: Points System: Sum-Based Aggregation
docs.text:
Why: - Simple, transparent scoring (points = effort) - Historical tracking via PointsLog for audit - Supports rolling window goals (7-day, 30-day)

Trade-off: No decay or weighting; old points count equally to recent points

docs.title: Frontend: React Query for State Management
docs.text:
Why: - Client-side caching reduces API calls - Automatic cache invalidation on mutations - Optimistic updates improve perceived performance - Handles loading and error states cleanly

Trade-off: Server-side state not shared across browser tabs (acceptable for household app)

docs.title: Architecture: Layered (Router → Service → Model → Database)
docs.text:
Why: - Clean separation of concerns - Testable business logic in services - API contracts defined in schemas - Easy to extend with new endpoints

Trade-off: More files/structure than monolithic approach

docs.title: Security Considerations
docs.text:
1. Authentication: JWT tokens with 365-day expiration
  2. Password: SHA256 pre-hash before bcrypt
  3. Token Blacklist: InvalidTokenList in DB for logout
  4. CORS: Configured for frontend origin
  5. Input Validation: Pydantic schemas validate all inputs
  6. Admin Actions: is_admin flag protects sensitive operations

docs.title: Performance Optimizations
docs.text:
1. React Query: Client-side caching of queries
  2. Query Invalidation: Precise cache invalidation after mutations
  3. Async Database: AsyncSession for non-blocking I/O
  4. Scheduler: Single background process for system events
  5. Database Indexes: unique_id, username, state, next_due (implicit)

docs.title: Future Scalability
docs.text:
• Multi-user: Already supports multiple Person records
  • Custom Themes: In-memory store, could migrate to database
  • Logging: ChoreLog ready, system events populated
  • API Clients: Can be extended beyond React frontend
  • Real-time Updates: WebSocket support could be added

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Docker & Docker Compose
  • (Optional) Domain name and SSL certificate for production

docs.title: Development Deployment
docs.text:
docker-compose up

This starts: - Frontend: http://localhost:3000 (Nginx reverse proxy) - Backend: http://localhost:8000 - API Docs: http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: Production Deployment
docs.text:
1. Update docker-compose.yml for production:
  2. Set ENVIRONMENT=production
  3. Change frontend to serve from /api proxy
  4. Enable HTTPS redirect

  5. Set proper CORS origins

  6. Build images: 

    docker-compose -f docker-compose.yml build

  7. Start services: 

    docker-compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d

  8. Verify health: 

    curl http://localhost:8000/health

docs.title: Backend Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Type Required Development Production Description DATABASE_URL string Yes postgresql+asyncpg://chores:chores@db/chores postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@db.example.com/chores PostgreSQL async connection string CORS_ORIGINS string Yes http://localhost:3000 https://yourdomain.com Frontend origin for CORS validation ENVIRONMENT string Yes development production Environment mode (affects logging, debug, etc.) LOG_LEVEL string No INFO INFO or WARNING Logging level (DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR) JWT_SECRET string Yes (prod) auto-generated your-secret-key-here Secret for JWT token signing JWT_EXPIRATION_DAYS int No 365 365 JWT token expiration in days

docs.title: Frontend Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Type Required Development Production Description VITE_API_URL string Yes http://backend:8000/api https://yourdomain.com/api Backend API base URL

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
Primary Database: PostgreSQL

Connection format: 

postgresql+asyncpg://username:password@host:5432/database_name

In docker-compose (development): 

db:
  image: postgres:16-alpine
  environment:
    POSTGRES_USER: chores
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: chores
    POSTGRES_DB: chores

In production: - Use external PostgreSQL instance - Update DATABASE_URL with production credentials - Ensure database user has CREATE/ALTER privileges for schema initialization - Backend auto-creates tables on startup via SQLAlchemy - No manual migrations needed (schema-first approach)

Schema Overview:

  • Person – Users (id, name, username, password_hash, is_admin, color, goals, points)
  • Chore – Task definitions (id, name, schedule_type, assignment_type, points, state)
  • PointsLog – Point transactions (id, person_id, points, chore_id, completed_at)
  • ChoreLog – Audit log (id, chore_id, person_id, action, timestamp)
  • Settings – System config (key, value)
  • TokenBlacklist – Invalidated tokens (token_jti, expires_at)

docs.title: HTTPS
docs.text:
1. Obtain SSL certificate (Let's Encrypt recommended)

  2. Mount cert in nginx container: 

    volumes:
  - /etc/letsencrypt/live/yourdomain:/etc/ssl/certs

  3. Configure nginx SSL: 

    server {
  listen 443 ssl;
  server_name yourdomain.com;
  ssl_certificate /etc/ssl/certs/fullchain.pem;
  ssl_certificate_key /etc/ssl/certs/privkey.pem;
  # ... rest of config
}

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
• JWT tokens expire after 365 days
  • Tokens stored in localStorage (frontend)
  • Token blacklist tracks logouts
  • Passwords hashed with bcrypt (after SHA256 pre-hash)

docs.title: CORS
docs.text:
Default allows all origins in development. For production:

# backend/app/main.py
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["https://yourdomain.com"],
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

docs.title: Backup Database
docs.text:
# Backup SQLite
docker-compose exec backend cp app.db app.db.backup

# Restore from backup
docker-compose exec backend cp app.db.backup app.db

docs.title: Backup Data
docs.text:
# Create backup archive
docker-compose exec backend tar czf app.db.tar.gz app.db

# Download backup
docker cp <container>:/app/app.db.tar.gz ./backup.tar.gz

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8000/health

docs.title: Logs
docs.text:
# Frontend logs
docker-compose logs frontend

# Backend logs
docker-compose logs backend -f

# All logs
docker-compose logs -f

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
Monitor via docker stats: 

docker stats

docs.title: Port Already in Use
docs.text:
# Find process using port
lsof -i :8000
lsof -i :3000

# Kill process
kill -9 <PID>

docs.title: PostgreSQL Connection Issues
docs.text:
# Verify PostgreSQL is running
docker-compose ps | grep db

# Check logs
docker-compose logs db

# Test connection from backend
docker-compose exec backend python -c \
  "from app.database import engine; \
   import asyncio; \
   asyncio.run(engine.connect())"

Common issues: - DATABASE_URL format incorrect (missing +asyncpg) - PostgreSQL password wrong - Database user missing CREATE privilege - Port 5432 not exposed or already in use - Network connectivity between containers

docs.title: Database Performance Issues
docs.text:
Slow queries: 

# Enable query logging in PostgreSQL
# Add to docker-compose.yml postgres environment:
POSTGRES_INIT_ARGS: "-c log_statement=all"

# Check logs
docker-compose logs db | grep query

Connection pool issues: 

# Check active connections
docker-compose exec db psql -U chores -d chores \
  -c "SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity;"

docs.title: CORS Errors
docs.text:
1. Check frontend VITE_API_URL points to backend (should match CORS_ORIGINS)
  2. Verify backend CORS middleware allows frontend origin in docker-compose.yml
  3. Check browser console Network tab for actual error (look at Preflight OPTIONS request)
  4. Production: ensure CORS_ORIGINS uses HTTPS if frontend is HTTPS

Fix: 

# docker-compose.yml
backend:
  environment:
    CORS_ORIGINS: "https://yourdomain.com"  # Must match frontend origin exactly

docs.title: Authentication Issues
docs.text:
1. Token not present:
  2. Check token in localStorage: localStorage.getItem('token')
  3. Verify login was successful (check network tab)

  4. Check browser accepts/sends cookies

  5. Invalid token:

  6. Verify token in Authorization header: Authorization: Bearer <token>
  7. Check token format (should be JWT, not just a string)

  8. Verify JWT_SECRET matches between deployments

  9. Token expired:

  10. Check token expiration: jwt.decode(token, options={"verify_signature": False})
  11. JWT tokens expire after 365 days, user must login again

  12. For long-lived sessions, consider refresh token pattern

  13. User no longer exists:

  14. Verify user still exists in database
  15. Check if user was deleted after login
  16. Verify username matches between frontend and backend

Debug auth issues: 

# Check token blacklist
docker-compose exec db psql -U chores -d chores \
  -c "SELECT * FROM token_blacklist;"

# Check active users
docker-compose exec db psql -U chores -d chores \
  -c "SELECT id, username, is_admin FROM person;"

docs.title: Container Startup Issues
docs.text:
# View startup logs
docker-compose logs backend --tail 100

# Common errors:
# "Connection refused" - database not ready
#   Solution: Wait for db container to be healthy, check logs
# "Address already in use" - port conflict
#   Solution: Change ports in docker-compose.yml or kill existing process
# "Schema mismatch" - database schema outdated
#   Solution: Backend auto-creates schema on startup, check db logs

docs.title: Network Issues Between Containers
docs.text:
# Test backend can reach database
docker-compose exec backend ping db

# Test frontend can reach backend
docker-compose exec frontend wget -O- http://backend:8000/health

docs.title: Horizontal Scaling
docs.text:
Use Docker Swarm or Kubernetes: 

# Docker Swarm
docker swarm init
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml chores

docs.title: Database Scaling
docs.text: For PostgreSQL with replication: 1. Set up primary/replica PostgreSQL 2. Update DATABASE_URL to primary 3. Configure read replicas for reporting queries

docs.title: Caching Layer
docs.text:
Add Redis for caching: 

services:
  redis:
    image: redis:latest
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"

Then implement caching in FastAPI: 

@app.get("/chores")
async def list_chores(cache: Redis = Depends(get_redis)):
    # Check cache first
    # Fall back to database

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
• Add indexes on frequently-queried columns
  • next_due and state benefit from indexes
  • Monitor slow queries with logging

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Enable service worker caching
  • Optimize bundle size (currently ~200KB)
  • Use CDN for static assets

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• Use async database queries (already done)
  • Connection pooling (SQLAlchemy handles this)
  • Consider caching for read-heavy workloads

docs.title: Regular Tasks
docs.text:
• Daily: Monitor logs for errors
  • Weekly: Backup database
  • Monthly: Review performance metrics
  • Quarterly: Update dependencies

docs.title: Updates
docs.text:
1. Pull latest code
  2. Run tests: npm test -- --run
  3. Build images: docker-compose build
  4. Stop old containers: docker-compose down
  5. Start new containers: docker-compose up -d
  6. Verify health: curl localhost:8000/health

docs.title: Zero-Downtime Updates
docs.text:
For critical deployments:

# Start new container
docker-compose up -d --scale backend=2

# When ready, remove old container
docker-compose down

docs.title: Steps to Recover
docs.text:
1. Stop containers: docker-compose down
  2. Restore database from backup
  3. Rebuild images: docker-compose build
  4. Start services: docker-compose up -d
  5. Verify: curl localhost:8000/health

docs.title: Data Loss Prevention
docs.text:
• Automatic daily backups
  • Test restore procedure monthly
  • Keep backups on separate system
  • Document all manual changes to database

docs.title: Pre-Deployment (Before Deploy)
docs.text:
• Database fully backed up
  • All tests passing locally (npm test -- --run)
  • Code reviewed and approved
  • Environment variables configured for production
  • SSL/HTTPS certificates obtained and valid
  • Disaster recovery plan documented and tested
  • Rollback procedure documented
  • All team members notified of deployment window

docs.title: During Deployment
docs.text:
• Monitor health endpoint during startup: curl https://yourdomain.com/health
  • Check backend logs for errors: docker logs <backend-container>
  • Verify frontend loads without errors (browser console check)
  • Test login with known user account
  • Verify API endpoints respond: curl https://yourdomain.com/api/people
  • Check database connectivity and query performance
  • Monitor system resources (CPU, memory, disk)

docs.title: Post-Deployment (After Deploy)
docs.text:
• Smoke test: Complete a full chore workflow
  • Verify data integrity (check person count, chore count)
  • Confirm backups still working
  • Review monitoring dashboards (if configured)
  • Check log aggregation system (if configured)
  • Verify users can access from multiple browsers
  • Document deployment completion and any issues
  • Keep backup of pre-deployment database for 7 days minimum

docs.title: Ongoing Production Operations
docs.text:
• CORS origins restricted to your domain only
  • Database backups scheduled and tested weekly
  • Monitoring/alerting configured for uptime
  • Log aggregation configured and reviewed regularly
  • Authentication tokens using secure SECRET key (not default)
  • Regular security updates (monthly review of dependencies)
  • Health checks monitored (set up uptime monitoring service)
  • Rate limiting enabled if public-facing
  • Change password for default admin account
  • Remove development/test data before going live

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js 16+ (for frontend)
  • Python 3.11+ (for backend)
  • Git

docs.title: Backend Setup
docs.text:
# Navigate to backend directory
cd backend

# Create virtual environment
python -m venv venv

# Activate virtual environment
# On macOS/Linux:
source venv/bin/activate
# On Windows:
venv\Scripts\activate

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Run development server
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

Swagger UI: http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: Frontend Setup
docs.text:
# Navigate to frontend directory
cd frontend

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Run development server
npm run dev

Development URL: http://localhost:5173

docs.title: First-Time Setup
docs.text:
1. Start backend (opens on :8000)
  2. Start frontend (opens on :5173)
  3. Frontend will redirect to setup page if no users exist
  4. Create first admin user through setup page
  5. Login with created credentials

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
Frontend: 

cd frontend
npm test                 # Watch mode
npm test -- --run      # Single run

Tests must pass before committing.

docs.title: Making Changes
docs.text:
1. Frontend Changes:
  2. Edit components/pages in src/
  3. Tests automatically re-run
  4. Ensure tests pass: npm test -- --run

  5. Commit only if tests pass (205/205)

  6. Backend Changes:

  7. Edit routers/services in app/
  8. Server auto-reloads with --reload
  9. Test via Swagger UI or API client
  10. Add appropriate logging to ChoreLog

  11. Ensure database migrations work

  12. Database Schema Changes:

  13. Add/modify fields in models.py
  14. Update corresponding schemas in schemas.py
  15. Tables auto-create on startup (see lifespan in main.py)
  16. Add migration scripts if needed for existing data

docs.title: Commit Guidelines
docs.text:
• Title: Short, imperative mood ("add feature", "fix bug", "refactor component")
  • Body: Explain why not what (code shows what)
  • Test Status: Include "Frontend tests: 205/205 passing ✓" if applicable
  • Co-authored: End with "Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 noreply@anthropic.com"

Example: 

feat: add theme deletion for custom themes

- Add DELETE /theme/delete/{theme_id} endpoint
- Prevent deletion of default themes (dark, light, ocean)
- Switch to dark theme if current is deleted
- Add confirmation modal in ThemeSettings component
- Add 3 tests for deletion functionality

Frontend tests: 205/205 passing ✓

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs.title: Adding a New Endpoint
docs.text:
1. Define Schema (in schemas.py): 

    class NewThingOut(BaseModel):
    id: int
    name: str
    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}

  2. Add Router Handler (in routers/): 

    @router.get("", response_model=list[NewThingOut], summary="List all things")
async def list_things(
    current_user: str = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Get all things. Optionally filtered by query params."""
    # Implementation

  3. Add Service Logic (if complex, in services/): 

    async def process_thing(thing: Thing, db: AsyncSession) -> Thing:
    # Business logic here
    await _log_action(thing, "processed", "user", db)
    db.add(thing)
    await db.commit()
    return thing

  4. Add to Main App (already done if using include_router):

  5. Routers are auto-included in main.py

  6. Document (in docs/API.md):

  7. Add endpoint documentation with example request/response

  8. Test (in Swagger UI):

  9. Verify endpoint works before committing

docs.title: Logging Actions
docs.text:
All user-visible actions should be logged:

await _log_action(
    chore=chore_obj,
    action="completed",           # Action type
    person="username",            # Username or "system"
    db=db,
    reassigned_to=None,          # Optional, for reassignment
)

docs.title: Component Structure
docs.text:
// src/components/MyComponent.tsx
import React, { useState } from "react";
import { useQuery, useMutation } from "@tanstack/react-query";
import { getThings, updateThing } from "../api/client";
import "./MyComponent.css";

interface Thing {
  id: number;
  name: string;
}

export default function MyComponent() {
  const { data: things = [], isLoading } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ["things"],
    queryFn: getThings,
  });

  const mutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: updateThing,
    onSuccess: () => {
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["things"] });
    },
  });

  if (isLoading) return <div>Loading...</div>;

  return (
    <div>
      {things.map(thing => (
        <div key={thing.id}>{thing.name}</div>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}

docs.title: Component Testing
docs.text:
// src/__tests__/MyComponent.test.jsx
import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react";
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query";
import MyComponent from "../components/MyComponent";
import * as client from "../api/client";

vi.mock("../api/client");

function wrap(ui) {
  const qc = new QueryClient({ defaultOptions: { queries: { retry: false } } });
  return render(<QueryClientProvider client={qc}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>);
}

describe("MyComponent", () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    vi.resetAllMocks();
    client.getThings.mockResolvedValue([]);
  });

  it("renders empty state", async () => {
    wrap(<MyComponent />);
    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText("Loading...")).not.toBeInTheDocument());
  });
});

docs.title: Styling
docs.text:
• Use CSS files (e.g., MyComponent.css)
  • Reference CSS variables: var(--bg), var(--accent), etc.
  • Mobile-first responsive design
  • Theme colors already defined in App.css

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
// In browser DevTools:
// - React DevTools extension
// - Redux DevTools (React Query tab)
// - Console for network requests
console.log("Debug:", thing);

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
# In code
import logging
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
logger.debug(f"Processing thing: {thing}")

# In terminal with --reload
# Server logs appear in terminal

docs.title: Performance Tips
docs.text:
1. React Query: Use proper query keys to avoid unnecessary fetches
  2. Memoization: Use useMemo for expensive computations
  3. Invalidation: Be specific with invalidateQueries (use queryKey prefix)
  4. Database: Use async queries, avoid N+1 problems
  5. Images: Optimize before uploading

docs.title: Security Checklist
docs.text:
• Validate all inputs (Pydantic handles backend)
  • Check auth on all endpoints (get_current_user dependency)
  • Prevent admin-only operations from regular users
  • Sanitize/escape any user-generated content
  • Use HTTPS in production (see DEPLOYMENT.md)

docs.title: Add a new field to Chore
docs.text:
1. Add to models.py Chore class: 

    new_field: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(Text, default="")

  2. Add to schemas.py ChoreOut: 

    new_field: str

  3. Add to ChoreCreate/ChoreUpdate if user-settable: 

    new_field: Optional[str] = None

  4. Database auto-creates column on next startup

docs.title: Add a new action type
docs.text:
1. Add constant to chore_service.py: 

    CHANGE_THING = "thing"

  2. Call in service function: 

    await _log_action(chore, CHANGE_THING, "system", db)

  3. Update docs/API.md to document new action type

docs.title: Create a new page
docs.text:
1. Create in src/pages/MyPage.tsx
  2. Import in src/App.jsx
  3. Add route in router setup
  4. Add navigation link in src/components/Sidebar.tsx
  5. Create tests in src/__tests__/MyPage.test.jsx

docs.title: Frontend tests failing
docs.text:
• Run npm test -- --run to see full output
  • Check test mocks are set up correctly
  • Ensure React Query queryClient is in test wrapper

docs.title: Backend startup issues
docs.text:
• Check requirements.txt installed: pip list
  • Verify Python 3.11+: python --version
  • Check port 8000 not in use: lsof -i :8000

docs.title: Database errors
docs.text:
• Delete backend/app.db to reset database
  • Check models.py has no syntax errors
  • Verify schema migrations don't break existing data

docs.title: Performance issues
docs.text:
• Check React Query DevTools (browser extension)
  • Profile with browser DevTools Perf tab
  • Check backend logs for slow queries
  • Verify indexes on frequently-queried columns

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• FastAPI Docs
  • React Docs
  • React Query Docs
  • SQLAlchemy Docs
  • Vitest Docs

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text: Welcome to Chores Web! This guide will help you get started and make the most of the app.

docs.title: What is Chores Web?
docs.text: Chores Web is a household chore management app that helps families and roommates organize, assign, and track responsibilities fairly. It supports flexible scheduling, point-based rewards, and keeps a complete audit log of all activities.

docs.title: Who Can Use This App?
docs.text:
• Families managing household chores across multiple members
  • Roommate groups sharing apartment or house responsibilities
  • Anyone wanting a fair, transparent way to distribute and track tasks

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Schedule chores by week, month, or custom intervals
  • Assign chores as open (anyone), rotating (fair rotation), or fixed (specific person)
  • Award points for completed chores and track progress
  • View complete history of all chore actions
  • Customize appearance with personal themes
  • Backup and restore your data

docs.title: Logging In
docs.text:
1. Open the app in your browser (provided URL from admin)
  2. Enter your username and password
  3. Click Login
  4. You'll see the Dashboard with an overview of your chores and points

docs.title: Dashboard Overview
docs.text: The main dashboard shows: - Your Points – Total points earned, 7-day and 30-day goals - Chores Due – Chores assigned to you or open for anyone - Quick Actions – Complete or skip chores - Leaderboard – Points ranking for all household members

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: Use the menu (top-left) to access: - Dashboard – Home page and overview - Chores – View and manage all chores - Points – Detailed points history and goals - Log – Complete audit trail of all actions - Settings – Your personal preferences - Admin (admin only) – Manage users and system settings

docs.title: View All Chores
docs.text:
1. Click Chores in the menu
  2. See all household chores with their:
  3. Name and description
  4. Schedule (weekly, monthly, etc.)
  5. Points awarded
  6. Current state (due or complete)
  7. Assigned person

docs.title: Complete a Chore
docs.text:
For chores assigned to you: 1. Find the chore in the list (or on Dashboard) 2. Click Complete 3. The chore is marked complete, you get the points! 4. Next occurrence is automatically scheduled

For open chores (anyone can complete): 1. Click the chore in the list 2. Click Complete 3. Chore is marked complete for you, points awarded

docs.title: Skip a Chore
docs.text: If you can't complete a chore: 1. Click the chore in the list 2. Click Skip 3. Chore moves to next cycle without awarding points 4. The next assigned person (if rotating) takes it next cycle

docs.title: Reassign a Chore
docs.text: If you need someone else to do the chore: 1. Click the chore 2. Click Reassign 3. Select the new person from the dropdown 4. Chore is reassigned, they'll see it next

docs.title: Creating Chores (Admin Only)
docs.text:
If you're an admin, you can create new chores:

  1. Go to Chores → Create New Chore
  2. Fill in:
  3. Name – What is the chore? (e.g., "Vacuum Living Room")
  4. Schedule – When does it repeat?
    • Weekly: Pick days (e.g., Monday, Wednesday, Friday)
    • Monthly: Pick date or weekday occurrence
    • Interval: Every N days
  5. Points – How many points for completing? (default 1)
  6. Assignment Type – How is it assigned?
    • Open – Anyone can complete it
    • Rotating – Cycles through eligible people
    • Fixed – Always the same person
  7. Eligible People – Who can be assigned (for rotating/fixed)
  8. Click Create

docs.title: Open Chores
docs.text:
• Anyone in the household can complete
  • Whoever completes first gets the points
  • Good for flexible tasks (take out trash, sweep kitchen)
  • No specific assignment needed

docs.title: Rotating Chores
docs.text:
• Cycles through a list of people
  • Each person does it when it's their turn
  • Fair distribution over time
  • Good for shared responsibility (mow lawn, clean bathroom)

Example: Lawn mowing rotates between Alex → Bailey → Charlie → Alex...

docs.title: Fixed Chores
docs.text:
• Same person always does it
  • Never rotates
  • Good for tasks needing specific skills or assigned to one person
  • Can be reassigned if needed

docs.title: How Points Work
docs.text:
• Earning: You get points when you complete a chore
  • Amount: Each chore awards a specific number of points (default 1)
  • Tracking: Your total points are shown on the Dashboard
  • History: See all point transactions in the Points page

Example: - Vacuum (5 points) – complete, you get +5 - Dishes (2 points) – complete, you get +2 - Total: 7 points

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
Set personal point goals to track progress:

7-Day Goal: Target points for the current week (Mon-Sun) - Helps with weekly motivation - Resets every Sunday

30-Day Goal: Target points for the current month - Longer-term tracking - Resets on the 1st of each month

docs.title: Checking Your Progress
docs.text:
1. Go to Dashboard or Points page
  2. See your current points and goal progress
  3. Compare with others on the leaderboard (if visible)

docs.title: Managing Users
docs.text:
Admins can create, modify, and remove users:

Create a user: 1. Go to Admin → Users 2. Click Add User 3. Enter name, username, password 4. Set as admin (if needed) 5. Click Create

Modify a user: 1. Go to Admin → Users 2. Find the user, click Edit 3. Update name, color, goals, or theme preference 4. Click Save

Delete a user: 1. Go to Admin → Users 2. Find the user, click Delete 3. All their chores and history remain (for record)

docs.title: Editing Chores (Admin)
docs.text:
1. Go to Chores
  2. Find the chore, click Edit
  3. Modify name, schedule, points, assignment
  4. Click Save

Deleting a chore: 1. Go to Chores 2. Find the chore, click Delete 3. Confirm the deletion (all history is kept)

docs.title: Export & Import Data
docs.text:
Backup your data: 1. Go to Settings → Data Management 2. Click Export 3. A JSON file downloads with all chores, people, settings 4. Save this file safely (Google Drive, cloud storage, etc.)

Restore from backup: 1. Go to Settings → Data Management 2. Click Import 3. Select your backup JSON file 4. Confirm (this replaces all current data) 5. Wait for import to complete

docs.title: System Settings
docs.text: Admins can configure: - Admin theme (applies to all users) - System name/title (optional) - Custom theme colors

docs.title: "I can't see a chore I'm supposed to do"
docs.text: Solutions: - Check Dashboard – it may not be due yet - Go to Chores → look for chores with "Your Turn" or your name - If rotating chore, your turn may not have started yet - If fixed chore, admin may have assigned it to someone else - Try refreshing the page (Ctrl+R or Cmd+R)

docs.title: "I completed the chore but didn't get points"
docs.text: Solutions: - Check Points page – the points should show up immediately - If not there, try refreshing (Ctrl+R) - If still missing, ask an admin – they can manually award points or check logs

docs.title: "The app logged me out unexpectedly"
docs.text: Solutions: - Log in again with your username and password - Tokens expire after 1 year – if it's been that long, this is normal - If you keep getting logged out quickly, ask admin (may be a connection issue)

docs.title: "I see an error message"
docs.text: Common errors: - "Network error" – Check internet connection - "Unauthorized" – You may not have permission for that action - "Not found" – The item may have been deleted - Contact an admin if the error persists

docs.title: "Why can't I create/delete chores?"
docs.text:
• Only admins can create or delete chores
  • Ask an admin for access or to create the chore for you

docs.title: "Can I change another person's password?"
docs.text:
• No – Only admins can reset passwords through the admin panel
  • Ask an admin to reset your password if forgotten

docs.title: "How do I see who did what?"
docs.text:
• Go to Log to see all chore actions (completion, skip, reassign, etc.)
  • Each entry shows who, what, when

docs.title: "Can I export my points history?"
docs.text:
• Export via Settings → Data Management includes your PointsLog
  • The JSON file has all transactions with timestamps
  • You can analyze it in Excel or a spreadsheet

docs.title: Personal Settings
docs.text:
1. Click your avatar (top-right) → Settings
  2. Modify:
  3. Color – Your personal color on the dashboard
  4. 7-Day Goal – Weekly point target
  5. 30-Day Goal – Monthly point target
  6. Theme – Light/dark mode or custom themes
  7. Click Save

docs.title: Change Your Password
docs.text:
1. Click your avatar → Settings
  2. Click Change Password
  3. Enter old password, new password, confirm
  4. Click Update

docs.title: Themes
docs.text:
Available themes: - Light (default white/bright) - Dark (dark background, white text) - Custom (admin or user-created themes with custom colors)

To change theme: 1. Go to Settings 2. Select a theme from the dropdown 3. Apply immediately

docs.title: Logout
docs.text:
1. Click your avatar (top-right)
  2. Click Logout
  3. You're logged out, must login again to use the app

docs.title: For Fair Rotation
docs.text:
• Use rotating chores for shared tasks
  • Make sure "Eligible People" includes everyone who should take turns
  • Check the rotation regularly to ensure fairness

docs.title: For Motivation
docs.text:
• Set realistic 7-day and 30-day goals
  • Check leaderboard occasionally for friendly competition
  • Celebrate reaching goals!

docs.title: For Household Efficiency
docs.text:
• Use fixed chores for specialized tasks (yard work, car washing)
  • Use open chores for quick tasks (sweep, wipe counters)
  • Use rotating for regular shared tasks (dishes, bathrooms)
  • Adjust points to reflect difficulty (quick tasks = 1-2 pts, complex = 5+ pts)

docs.title: Troubleshooting Tips
docs.text:
• Stuck? Check the Log page to understand what's happening
  • Confused about a chore? Click it to see full details, schedule, and history
  • Need help? Ask your admin or check this guide again
  • Found a bug? Report it to the development team

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• Admin questions – Contact your household admin
  • How-to questions – Check this guide first
  • Technical issues – Check the Troubleshooting section above
  • Feature requests – Talk to your admin

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
REST API for managing household chores, assignments, and tracking. Built with FastAPI.

Base URL: /api Authentication: Bearer token (JWT) Default Port: 8000 Interactive Docs: http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All endpoints except /auth/setup-status require Bearer token authentication.

Authorization: Bearer <access_token>

docs.title: POST /auth/login
docs.text:
Login with username and password.

Request: 

{
  "username": "string",
  "password": "string"
}

Response (200): 

{
  "access_token": "string",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "user": {
    "username": "string",
    "is_admin": boolean
  }
}

docs.title: GET /auth/setup-status
docs.text:
Check if system is initialized (no users exist).

Response (200): 

{
  "is_setup": boolean
}

docs.title: POST /auth/logout
docs.text:
Logout and invalidate current token.

Response (200): 

{
  "message": "Logged out successfully"
}

docs.title: PUT /auth/password
docs.text:
Change password for current user.

Request: 

{
  "old_password": "string",
  "new_password": "string"
}

Response (200): 

{
  "message": "Password changed successfully"
}

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
All errors follow this format:

{
  "detail": "string"
}
Code Meaning 200 Success 201 Created 204 No content (delete) 400 Bad request / validation error 401 Unauthorized — missing or invalid token 403 Forbidden — insufficient permissions 404 Not found 500 Internal server error

docs.title: Chore Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "unique_id": "string",
  "name": "string",
  "schedule_type": "weekly|monthly|interval",
  "schedule_config": {},
  "assignment_type": "open|rotating|fixed",
  "eligible_people": ["string"],
  "assignee": "string|null",
  "points": integer,
  "state": "due|complete",
  "disabled": boolean,
  "next_due": "YYYY-MM-DD|null",
  "current_assignee": "string|null",
  "age": integer|null,
  "schedule_summary": "string",
  "next_assignee": "string|null"
}

docs.title: Weekly
docs.text:
{
  "days": [0, 2, 5],
  "every_other_week": false
}

docs.title: Monthly (by date)
docs.text:
{
  "day_of_month": 15
}

docs.title: Monthly (by weekday)
docs.text:
{
  "weekday_occurrence": {
    "week": 1,
    "weekday": 0
  }
}

docs.title: Interval
docs.text:
{
  "days": 7
}

docs.title: With Conditions
docs.text:
{
  "days": [0, 1, 2],
  "conditions": [
    { "type": "even_days" },
    { "type": "odd_days" },
    { "type": "weekdays", "days": [1, 2, 3] }
  ],
  "condition_failure": "skip"
}

docs.title: GET /chores
docs.text:
List all chores.

Response (200): Array of chore objects

docs.title: GET /chores/{chore_id}
docs.text:
Get single chore by unique_id.

Response (200): Chore object Response (404): Not found

docs.title: POST /chores
docs.text:
Create new chore.

Request: 

{
  "name": "string",
  "schedule_type": "weekly|monthly|interval",
  "schedule_config": {},
  "assignment_type": "open|rotating|fixed",
  "eligible_people": ["string"],
  "assignee": "string|null",
  "points": integer,
  "disabled": boolean
}

Response (201): Created chore with computed fields

docs.title: PUT /chores/{chore_id}
docs.text:
Update chore. All fields optional.

Response (200): Updated chore

docs.title: DELETE /chores/{chore_id}
docs.text:
Delete chore and all associated points history.

Response (204): No content

docs.title: POST /chores/{chore_id}/complete
docs.text:
Mark chore completed, award points.

Request: 

{
  "completed_by": "string|null"
}

Response (200): Updated chore

docs.title: POST /chores/{chore_id}/skip
docs.text:
Skip chore (no points), calculate next due date.

Response (200): Updated chore

docs.title: POST /chores/{chore_id}/skip-reassign
docs.text:
Skip and reassign to another person.

Request: 

{
  "assignee": "string|null"
}

Response (200): Updated chore

docs.title: POST /chores/{chore_id}/reassign
docs.text:
Reassign chore to different person.

Request: 

{
  "assignee": "string"
}

Response (200): Updated chore

docs.title: POST /chores/{chore_id}/mark-due
docs.text:
Manually mark chore as due (skip to next cycle).

Response (200): Updated chore

docs.title: GET /config
docs.text:
Get system configuration.

Response (200): 

{
  "admin_theme": "string"
}

docs.title: PUT /config
docs.text:
Update system configuration. Admin only.

Request: 

{
  "admin_theme": "string|null"
}

Response (200): Updated config

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Health check.

Response (200): 

{
  "status": "ok"
}

docs.title: Export
docs.text: Export system configuration data for backup and data transfer purposes.

docs.title: GET /export/config
docs.text:
Export all configuration data (people, chores, settings).

Authentication: Required (Bearer token)

Response (200): 

{
  "people": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "string",
      "username": "string",
      "is_admin": boolean,
      "color": "#RRGGBB",
      "goal_7d": integer,
      "goal_30d": integer,
      "points": integer,
      "preferred_theme": "string|null"
    }
  ],
  "chores": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "unique_id": "string",
      "name": "string",
      "schedule_type": "weekly|monthly|interval",
      "schedule_config": {},
      "assignment_type": "open|rotating|fixed",
      "eligible_people": ["string"],
      "assignee": "string|null",
      "points": integer,
      "disabled": boolean
    }
  ],
  "settings": [
    {
      "key": "string",
      "value": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Notes: - Exports complete configuration data (all people, chores, settings) - Does not include history logs (ChoreLog, PointsLog) - Does not include computed/transient fields (next_due, current_assignee, age) - Use for backups or data migration to another instance - Any authenticated user can export

Example Use Case: 

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  http://localhost:8000/api/export/config \
  > backup.json

docs.title: Import
docs.text: Import previously exported configuration data to restore or migrate settings.

docs.title: POST /import/config
docs.text:
Import configuration data (people, chores, settings) from backup.

Authentication: Required (Bearer token)

Request: 

{
  "people": [
    {
      "name": "string",
      "username": "string",
      "color": "#RRGGBB",
      "goal_7d": integer,
      "goal_30d": integer,
      "preferred_theme": "string|null"
    }
  ],
  "chores": [
    {
      "unique_id": "string",
      "name": "string",
      "schedule_type": "weekly|monthly|interval",
      "schedule_config": {},
      "assignment_type": "open|rotating|fixed",
      "eligible_people": ["string"],
      "assignee": "string|null",
      "points": integer,
      "disabled": boolean
    }
  ],
  "settings": [
    {
      "key": "string",
      "value": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Response (200): 

{
  "message": "Import successful",
  "counts": {
    "people_imported": integer,
    "chores_imported": integer,
    "settings_imported": integer
  }
}

Response (400): Bad request / validation error 

{
  "detail": "string"
}

Response (401): Unauthorized 

{
  "detail": "Not authenticated"
}

Notes: - DESTRUCTIVE: Replaces all existing data (people, chores, settings) - Only authenticated users can import - File should match export format from GET /export/config - Validates all required fields before import - On failure, original data is preserved - Does not import history logs (PointsLog, ChoreLog) — these are not included in exports - Recommended to backup before importing

Example Use Case: 

curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @backup.json \
  http://localhost:8000/api/import/config

Warning: This operation is destructive. Always backup your data before importing. Test imports in a development environment first.

docs.title: GET /log
docs.text:
Chore action log with optional filters.

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Description person string Filter by person chore_id string Filter by chore action string completed, skipped, reassigned, marked_due, marked_due_by_schedule start_date YYYY-MM-DD Filter from date end_date YYYY-MM-DD Filter to date

Response (200): 

[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "chore_id": "string",
    "chore_name": "string",
    "person": "string",
    "action": "string",
    "timestamp": "ISO8601",
    "reassigned_to": "string|null"
  }
]

docs.title: Person Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "string",
  "username": "string",
  "is_admin": boolean,
  "color": "#RRGGBB",
  "goal_7d": integer,
  "goal_30d": integer,
  "points": integer,
  "preferred_theme": "string|null"
}

docs.title: GET /people
docs.text:
List all people.

Response (200): Array of person objects

docs.title: POST /people
docs.text:
Create new person. Admin only.

Request: 

{
  "name": "string",
  "username": "string",
  "password": "string|null"
}

Response (201): Created person

docs.title: PUT /people/{username}
docs.text:
Update person settings. Admin or self only.

Request: 

{
  "color": "#RRGGBB|null",
  "goal_7d": integer|null,
  "goal_30d": integer|null,
  "preferred_theme": "string|null"
}

Response (200): Updated person

docs.title: DELETE /people/{username}
docs.text:
Delete person. Admin only — cannot delete self.

Response (204): No content

docs.title: GET /points
docs.text:
Leaderboard — points earned in last 30 days.

Response (200): 

[
  {
    "person": "string",
    "points_7d": integer,
    "points_30d": integer
  }
]

docs.title: GET /points/summary
docs.text:
Summary stats for all people.

Response (200): Same as /points

docs.title: GET /points/{person}
docs.text:
Full points history for a specific person.

Response (200): 

[
  {
    "person": "string",
    "points": integer,
    "chore_id": "string",
    "completed_at": "ISO8601"
  }
]

docs.title: GET /points/stats/{person}
docs.text:
Aggregated stats for a specific person.

Response (200): 

{
  "person": "string",
  "points_7d": integer,
  "points_30d": integer
}

docs.title: Theme Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": "string",
  "name": "string",
  "colors": {
    "bg": "#RRGGBB",
    "surface": "#RRGGBB",
    "accent": "#RRGGBB",
    "success": "#RRGGBB",
    "warning": "#RRGGBB",
    "danger": "#RRGGBB"
  }
}

docs.title: GET /theme/list
docs.text:
List all themes — defaults and custom.

Response (200): Array of theme objects

docs.title: GET /theme/current
docs.text:
Get current user's preferred theme.

Response (200): Theme object

docs.title: POST /theme/set/{theme_id}
docs.text:
Set current user's preferred theme.

Response (200): Theme object

docs.title: POST /theme/save
docs.text:
Create new custom theme.

Request: 

{
  "name": "string",
  "colors": {
    "bg": "#RRGGBB",
    "surface": "#RRGGBB",
    "accent": "#RRGGBB",
    "success": "#RRGGBB",
    "warning": "#RRGGBB",
    "danger": "#RRGGBB"
  }
}

Response (200): Created theme

docs.title: DELETE /theme/delete/{theme_id}
docs.text:
Delete custom theme. Cannot delete defaults.

Response (204): No content Response (400): Cannot delete default theme
